economic_notes: COVID-19 pandemic significantly impacted operations including hotel, casino, and convenience store revenues. Received $7,724,725 in additional ISC COVID-19 funding. Flood damage recovery receivable of $1,051,280 from 2013 flooding still outstanding. Heritage Trust Capital Account had $55.4M transferred post year-end to Ozija Thiha Legacy Trust. Treaty Land Entitlement negotiations ongoing. Oil and gas royalties administered by Government of Canada under Indian Oil and Gas Act. Stoney Nakoda Economic Development Trust holds investments and has made loans of $8,061,708 to Nation entities. Multiple settlement trusts established for Ghost Lake and United Church settlements.
notes: Fiscal year 2021, not 2024. Auditor firm name not clearly identified - signed as Chartered Professional Accountants in Edmonton. Large consolidated entity with 45+ subsidiaries all fully consolidated including casinos, hotels, convenience stores, gas bars, telecom, housing, health services, and development corporations. No government business enterprises - all entities fully consolidated. Capital band funds line is negative (-$1,226,125) representing net position after deferrals. COVID-19 had significant impact on hotel, casino, and retail revenues. Loan covenant breach with CorpFinance International Limited - waiver obtained. CMHC replacement reserve underfunded at year end. Post year-end $55.4M transfer from Heritage Trust to Ozija Thiha Legacy Trust is a major event. Accumulated surplus per balance sheet is $321,869,996 but statement of operations shows $322,925,872 - difference due to remeasurement losses classification. ISC funding calculated as Fixed + Set + Flex = $60,416,129. Total government transfers includes ISC + CMHC + HRD + Health Canada + Provincial = approximately $83,869,657. Segmented revenue note was cut off in source text.
